retry_reason: free_extent_buffer always succeeds and there is no failure path; the function is a cleanup operation that does not need retrying.
contract_summary: Put wrapper: releases all references to extent buffers held in the path by calling free_extent_buffer (a put) on each non-NULL node, and unlocks any held locks. After the call, the path is cleared (all slots zero, nodes NULL, locks zero) and must not be accessed until re-initialized (e.g., via btrfs_init_path). The caller must ensure no further use of the released extent buffers, and must not call this function again on the same path without obtaining new references. This function is typically called at the end of a btrfs operation to safely discard the path's state.
